questions.question: What may happen if an Accounting Officer in a self-accounting unit fails to comply with the Financial Regulations?
options: No consequence on the ground of self-accounting status.
questions.explanation: Failure by an Accounting Officer in a self-accounting unit to comply with the Financial Regulations can attract disciplinary action and surcharge because the officer remains personally accountable for compliance.
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: Who takes disciplinary action against a driver who causes an accident through negligence?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Accounting Officer.). Financial Regulation 2022 states that the Accounting Officer is in charge for taking disciplinary action.
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the role of the Chairman of the Board of a Parastatal" according to official standards?
options: To supervise the day-to-day running of the parastatal.
To serve as the Accounting Officer of the parastatal.
To preside over Board meetings and maintain order during deliberations.
To represent the parastatal in all international forums.
questions.explanation: The Chairman of the Board presides over Board meetings and ensures that discussions remain orderly and focused on the Board's responsibilities.
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: Under Financial Regulation 1603(b), which group must receive accounting instructions from the Accounting Officer?
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1603(b) requires the Accounting Officer to issue accounting instructions to all officers within the unit, not just senior staff or outside bodies.
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: What type of reporting must the Accounting Officer ensure is accurate for management and control purposes?
options: Comprehensive financial reports, including budget-performance reporting.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Comprehensive financial reports including budget performance.). The Accounting Officer is in charge for budget proposals, defense, and monthly/quarterly budget performance statements, all indicating comprehensive financial reporting. 272 Section F Part 1: Financial Regulation: Internal Audit
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: According to Financial Regulation 1706, what type of report should the Internal Auditor submit to the Accounting Officer?
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1706 requires the Internal Auditor to submit periodic audit reports to the Accounting Officer.
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the main aim of Chapter 19 of the Financial Regulations" according to official standards?
options: To define the roles of Accounting Officers.
To outline the pension scheme in the Federal Public Service.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To outline the pension scheme in the Federal Public Service.). Chapter 19 is titled "Pension Scheme in the Federal Public Service."
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: Who is responsible under the Constitution for auditing and reporting on the public accounts of the Federation?
questions.explanation: The Auditor-General for the Federation is the constitutional officer responsible for auditing and reporting on the public accounts of the federation.
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: What does the term Sub-Accounting Officer mean?
options: An officer entrusted with the receipt, custody, and disbursement of public money and required to keep a cash book.
An officer who prepares financial reports for the Accountant-General.
The Head of Finance and Accounts.
An officer who audits the accounts of a ministry.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (An officer entrusted with receipt, custody, and disbursement of public money, demand to keep a cash book.). Financial Regulation 115(1) defines "Sub-Accounting Officer" as "an officer who is entrusted with the receipt, custody, and disbursement of public money and who is demand to keep one of the recognised Cash books."
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "a cash advance-holder" according to official standards?
options: An officer entrusted with disbursing public money for which vouchers cannot immediately be presented to a Sub-Accounting Officer, and who keeps a cash book.
An officer who approves all capital expenditure.
An officer who manages the ministry's main bank account.
An officer responsible for auditing revenue collections.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (An officer entrusted with the disbursement of public money for which vouchers cannot be immediately presented to a Sub-Accounting Officer, and who keeps a cash book.). Financial Regulation 120 defines "imprest-holder" as "an officer other than a Sub-Accounting officer who is entrusted with the disbursement of public money, for which vouchers cannot be presented immediately to a Sub-Accounting Officer and who is necessitate to keep a cash book."
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: When a Ministry or Department is constituted as a Self Accounting Unit, who is charged with installing and maintaining its accounting system?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 122 places responsibility for the installation and maintenance of the accounting system of a Self Accounting Unit on the Accounting Officer.
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: What does the Annual General Warrant permit the Accountant-General to do?
options: Only to make payments for personal emoluments.
To transfer funds from the Contingencies Fund.
To issue funds for disbursement for personal emoluments and other services provided in the Annual Estimates.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To issue funds to disbursement for personal emoluments and other services provided in the Annual Estimates.). Financial Regulation 304 states: "The General Warrant authorises the Accountant-General to issue funds to disbursement for the personal emoluments and other services provided in the Annual Estimates..."
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: which option aligns with the meaning of the definition of an "cash advance-holder"?
options: An officer who approves all capital expenditure.
An officer in charge for auditing revenue collections. Q
An officer entrusted with the disbursement of public money for which vouchers cannot be immediately presented to a Sub-Accounting Officer, and who keeps a cash book.
An officer who manages the ministry's main bank account.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (An officer entrusted with the disbursement of public money for which vouchers cannot be immediately presented to a Sub-Accounting Officer, and who keeps a cash book.). Financial Regulation 120 defines "cash advance-holder" as "an officer other than a Sub-Accounting officer who is entrusted with the disbursement of public money, for which vouchers cannot be presented immediately to a Sub-Accounting Officer and who is necessitate to keep a cash book."
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: In a Self Accounting Unit, who is charged with installing and maintaining a proper system of accounts?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 122 states that the Accounting Officer is responsible for installing and maintaining a proper accounting system in the unit.
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: What document empowers other officers to incur expenditure and sign vouchers on behalf of an Accounting Officer?
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 405 provides that an Accounting Officer may empower other officers by issuing an Authority to Incur Expenditure (A.I.E.).
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: What must accompany an Authority to Incur Expenditure issued by an Accounting Officer?
options: A copy of the Annual Estimates.
A list of all previous expenditures.
A letter of approval from the Minister of Finance.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 405 requires an Authority to Incur Expenditure to be supported by appropriate cash backing.
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: Who must ensure that the required vote-book entries are made before payments are approved?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Officers empowered to incur expenditure.). Financial Regulation 407 states: "Officers empowered to incur expenditure must secure by adding their initials against the. relevant entry that the requisite vote book entries are made before the payments are authorised by their signatures to the payment vouchers."
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: If an unauthorized payment arises from an incorrect certificate or entry on a voucher, who must be surcharged?
options: The certifying officer or the Sub-Accounting Officer.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The certifying officer or the Sub-Accounting Officer.). Financial Regulation 409 states: "the certifying officer or the sub-accounting officer as provided under Regulation 420 must be held accountable and surcharged for the amount involved."
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the minimum rank authorized to sign disbursement vouchers" according to official standards?
options: Senior Executive Officer (Accounts) or Accountant I.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 411 states that officers authorized to sign payment vouchers must not be below the rank of Accountant I or Senior Executive Officer (Accounts). The item therefore tests the minimum authorized rank for signing disbursement vouchers.
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: Who provides specimen signatures of officers empowered to sign payment vouchers to the internal checking section, internal audit unit, and Paying Officer?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Accounting Officer.). Financial Regulation 411 states: "The Accounting Officer must furnish the Internal checking section, internal audit unit and the Paying Officer with the specimen signatures along with the list of relevant votes chargeable."
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: What should be done if a discrepancy is found in schedules of payments and adjustments from the Accountant-General?
options: It should be corrected by the Accounting Officer.
It should be ignored if minor.
It should be brought immediately to the notice of the Accountant-General.
It should be reported only to the Auditor-General.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (It should be immediately brought to the notice of the Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 408 states: "Any discrepancy which such examinations may disclose should immediately be brought to the notice of the Accountant-General."
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: which role must approve any material variation of an existing accounting procedure or a proposed new accounting procedure?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts. □
The Accountant-General after consultation with the Auditor-General.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Accountant-General after consultation with the Auditor-General.). Financial Regulation 504 states: "Any material variation of an existing accounting procedure, or a proposed accounting procedure to be introduced into a new office, must be approved by the Accountant-General after consultation with the Auditor-General."
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: Who is required to acquaint themselves with the Financial Regulations, the Finance (Control and Management) Act, and the constitutional provisions on public finance?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Public Officers.). The Preface states: "Public Officers, are enjoined to acquaint themselves with these Regulations, the Finance (Control and Management) Act, Cap. 144 Laws of the federation of Nigeria 1990, as well as the relevant Sections of the Constitution dealing with public finance, for proper guidance."
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: responsibility for is accountable for maintaining a payment voucher register and confirming the accuracy of entries before signing a voucher belongs to which role?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Officer controlling the vote.). Financial Regulation 605 states: "Officer controlling vote must keep a disbursement voucher register... Under no circumstances must he sign a voucher until it has been entered in the register and has accordingly confirmed the accuracy of the entries therein."
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: In the revenue-allocation formula referenced in this item, what percentage goes to the Federal Government?
questions.explanation: In the revenue-allocation formula used for this item, the Federal Government receives 52.68% of the allocation.

questions.question: What outcome were the President's budget directives intended to achieve?
options: To reduce poverty, generate wealth, and create jobs.
questions.explanation: The budget directives were framed around reducing poverty, generating wealth, and creating jobs through government policy and expenditure planning.

subcategories.name: International & Regional Affairs
subcategories.description: Regional and global affairs relevant to Nigeria.

questions.question: An Officer traveling abroad for a short period is eligible for which allowance
questions.explanation: Officers shall be eligible for appropriate estacode allowance at the prevailing rates when they undertake special duty abroad or are on a delegation or visit to an organization abroad for a short period.

questions.question: If an officer is required to proceed to a foreign country on duty or an approved course of instruction in a cold or temperate climate, they are entitled to which allowance
questions.explanation: An Officer who is required to proceed to a foreign country on duty or on an approved course of instruction will be eligible for a warm clothing allowance.

questions.question: If the donor providing external training also provides free boarding and lodging, the officer concerned shall be entitled to what percentage of their appropriate estacode for the whole duration of the course
questions.explanation: Where the donor providing the training as a form of technical assistance to Nigeria also provides free boarding and lodging, the Officer concerned shall be entitled to 10 per cent of his appropriate estacode for the whole duration of the course.

questions.question: What benefit applies when a Foreign Service officer is injured on official duty and becomes incapacitated?
options: Full emolument until discharge from sick leave or permanent invalidation.
Pension payment only under routine rules.
questions.explanation: Where a Foreign Service officer is incapacitated by an injury sustained on official duty, full emolument continues until discharge from sick leave or permanent invalidation.

questions.question: When an officer is on official assignment outside Nigeria, when is the spouse entitled to free air passage?
options: When the assignment lasts at least nine months.
questions.explanation: The spouse is entitled to free air passage only where the officer's official duty outside Nigeria is for a period not less than nine months.

questions.question: What is prohibited concerning domestic staff of an entitled Officer at an overseas post, according to Foreign Service Regulations
options: Conversion of domestic staff to locally recruited staff.
questions.explanation: Conversion of domestic staff of an entitled Officer shall not be converted to locally recruited staff.

questions.question: Who must approve an officer's official visit while on leave outside Nigeria
options: Prior approval of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Before an Officer on leave outside Nigeria undertakes official visit, prior approval of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation shall be obtained.

questions.question: What is the entitlement for a Head of Diplomatic Mission regarding domestic staff
options: One cook and two security guards, under the applicable service framework.
One cook and two stewards; two security guards; and two drivers.
Three servants only, in line with formal public service practice.
Local staff only, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: The Head of Mission shall be entitled to the following domestic staff: one cook and two stewards; two security guards; and two drivers.

questions.question: The contribution of an Officer to secondary education of each child at an overseas post is what percentage of the cost (boarding and tuition inclusive)
questions.explanation: The contribution of an Officer to secondary education in respect of each child shall be 15 per cent of the cost (boarding and tuition inclusive).

questions.question: An officer serving in an overseas post may spend up to what fraction of his annual leave entitlement overseas
questions.explanation: An Officer serving in an overseas post may, subject to the exigencies of the Service, be permitted within any calendar year to spend up to half of his annual leave entitlement for that year as casual leave overseas.

questions.question: Which protocol is used for secure data transmission over the web
questions.explanation: HTTPS uses SSL/TLS to encrypt web traffic [111-113].

questions.question: The second-largest continent in the world is:
questions.explanation: Africa is the second largest continent in the world (cited in previous current affairs discussion). [Source not present in new excerpts, relying on established conversational fact].

questions.question: Which allowance covers lodging and per diem for officers on duty outside Nigeria
questions.explanation: Estacode Allowance is eligible for officers undertaking special duty or courses abroad.

questions.question: When an officer is seconded to the service of an approved international organization, what does that arrangement involve?
options: Temporary release for a specified period.
questions.explanation: Secondment is the temporary release of an officer to the service of another approved body or recognized international organization for a specified period. The item therefore tests the administrative meaning of secondment in service practice.

questions.question: An Officer who is on Leave of Absence on grounds of Public Policy (e.g., to join a spouse abroad) is granted what status regarding their service continuity
questions.explanation: Leave of Absence on grounds of Public Policy may be granted to a Foreign Service Officer who is also a Public Officer, to enable him join his spouse on posting abroad, granted as leave without pay.
questions.chapter: Leave on Grounds of Public Policy

questions.question: The two branches of the Nigerian Foreign Service are Branch A (Diplomatic) and Branch B (Non-Diplomatic). Officers who maintain proficiency in a language below standard are encouraged to do what
options: Take language lessons at Government expense.
questions.explanation: Officers who maintain proficiency in language lower standard or higher standard shall qualify for a one-off payment of 5 percent or 10 percent of Consolidated Foreign Service Allowance respectively.

questions.question: Who provides the detailed instructions and allowances regarding overseas duty or course of instruction
options: The Officer's Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
The FCSC, within established administrative procedures.
The Minister of Education, in line with formal public service practice.
The Ministry of Finance, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: An Officer required to undertake duty or a course of instruction outside Nigeria must be furnished with detailed instructions regarding such duties or course and be informed in advance and in writing of the allowances and traveling facilities for which he will be eligible for at Government expense; by his Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.

questions.question: If an officer serving overseas requires medical treatment that exceeds Chapter 13 requirements, what condition must be satisfied for Government to bear the expense
options: The officer must pay 50% of the cost.
The officer must obtain prior medical certification that they urgently need treatment outside Nigeria.
The officer must be GL 17, in line with formal public service practice.
The officer must have served 20 years.
questions.explanation: Government will bear the expenses.. provided that a Nigerian Officer urgently needs a specialist attention outside that country, certified by the Head of Mission based on medical opinion.

questions.question: When was the FIFA World Cup hosted by Qatar
questions.explanation: Qatar hosted the FIFA World Cup in 2022, making 2022 the correct year among the options given.

questions.question: For sea travel, what is the maximum container size entitlement for an Officer on SGL. 13 and above
questions.explanation: Officer on SGL. 13 and above shall be entitled to a maximum of 40 foot container.

questions.question: The **PSR** mandates that all officers must be placed on the **IPPIS** platform within what period of assumption of duty
questions.explanation: On first appointment, emolument, shall, as a general rule be paid as from the date of assumption of duty. All Officers shall be placed on IPPIS platform within two months of assumption of duty.

questions.question: Who must approve the deployment of staff in the Nigerian Civil Service
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: Appointments to offices in the Federal Civil Service are made on the authority of the Federal Civil Service Commission. Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices are authorized to appoint candidates where powers have been delegated.

questions.question: An officer on duty visit outside Nigeria is eligible to receive Estacode Allowance. What additional allocation is given if the officer incurs delegation or entertainment expense abroad
options: A special allocation authorized for that purpose.
questions.explanation: When it seems likely that a delegation or any individual Officer going abroad will have to incur expenditure on entertainment, a special allocation will be authorized for that purpose.

questions.question: The **FCSC** makes appointments to posts graded **GL.07**–17. The deadline for application submissions is six weeks from the date of advertisement placement in how many national newspapers
questions.explanation: The application notice is placed in three national newspapers and the Commission's website, so three newspapers is the correct count.

questions.question: An officer who is injured in the course of official duty and is permanently invalided will retire in accordance with which rule?
questions.explanation: An officer who is permanently invalided after injury in the course of official duty retires in accordance with Rule 120244.

questions.question: Which allowance is granted to enable Officers pay for lodging and feeding expenses during approved official tours within Nigeria
questions.explanation: Duty Tour Allowance is granted to enable Officers pay for lodging and feeding expenses during approved official tours.

questions.question: What is the penalty if an officer fails to comply with instructions delivered to him at his address within seven days of being placed on Interdiction
options: Deemed as absent from duty without leave.
questions.explanation: If he fails to comply with the instructions delivered to him at such address within seven days of such delivery, he will be regarded as absent from duty without leave.

questions.question: An officer proceeding or returning from an overseas posting is entitled to free air passage or sea passage for himself, his wife, and how many children
questions.explanation: An Officer proceeding or returning from an overseas posting.. is eligible for free air passage or sea passage as laid down in the Public Service Rules or extant Circulars for self, wife and four children.

questions.question: What compensation applies when a Foreign Service officer is injured on official duty and later discharged from sick leave or permanently invalided?
options: Full emolument until discharge or permanent invalidation.
Half salary under routine administrative practice.
questions.explanation: Where the officer is incapacitated by injury sustained on official duty, full emolument continues until discharge from sick leave or permanent invalidation.

questions.question: Which of the following bodies is NOT listed as a stakeholder for the National Council on Establishments (NCE)
questions.explanation: NCE stakeholders include FCSC, National Assembly Service Commission, and National Judicial Service Commission, but not Federal Ministry of Information.

questions.question: The National Council on Establishments (NCE) was established in what year
questions.explanation: The National Council on Establishments is traced in the source material to 1957, so that year is the correct answer rather than the later constitutional or civil-service milestones listed as distractors.

questions.question: What is the core function of the National Council on Establishments (NCE)
options: Approving the national budget, under the applicable service framework.
Approving the appointment of Ministers, within established administrative procedures.
Ensuring periodic review/update of the Schemes of Service and approving new cadres.
Overseeing discipline of all public officers, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: Functions of the National Council on Establishment: ensures periodic review/update of the Schemes of Service; approves new cadres..

questions.question: Who provides security guidelines for virtual meetings in line with **Rule 090104**'
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
The Federal Ministry of Communications and Digital Economy.
questions.explanation: Security of Government data/information.. shall be conducted in line with the Guidelines prescribed from time to time by the Federal Ministry of Communications and Digital Economy.

questions.question: What follows if an officer fails to submit representations on unsatisfactory conduct within the specified time?
questions.explanation: If the officer fails to submit representations within the allowed time, the appropriate sanction may be invoked on the basis of the unanswered allegation.

questions.question: What must officers avoid doing with historical manuscripts or other documents of public interest discovered in the course of official duty?
options: Reporting their existence to the Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office for preservation.
Forwarding them for examination and preservation.
Documenting their existence in line with official procedure.
questions.explanation: Officers must not appropriate historical manuscripts or documents of public interest for personal use; instead, their existence should be reported so that examination and preservation can follow.

questions.question: When an Officer's increment has been withheld, the **FCSC** may grant one or more **special increments** at a subsequent incremental date to mitigate what
options: The lasting effects of the withheld increment.
questions.explanation: The FCSC may at a subsequent incremental date grant one or more special increments having the effects of raising the salary of the Officer to or towards the level it would have reached if the previous withholding has not occurred.

questions.question: All decisions on deferment or withholding of increment and stoppage of salaries must be communicated to the officer concerned within what period
questions.explanation: All decisions on deferment or withholding of increment and stoppage of salaries shall be communicated.. to the Officer concerned within two weeks.

questions.question: How do the Public Service Rules define recruitment?
options: Any filling of vacancies regardless of service status.
Hiring of new staff through any workflow.
Appointment workflow in the public service generally.
Filling vacancies by appointing persons not already in the Federal Public Service.
questions.explanation: Under the PSR, recruitment means filling vacancies by appointing persons who are not already in the Public Service of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: What are financial authorities under the Financial Regulations?
options: Legal instruments empowering and guiding public officers in government financial transactions.
Documents outlining the budget allocation for each ministry.
Guidelines for personal investments of public officers.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Legal instruments empowering and guiding public officers in government financial transactions.). Financial Regulation 101 states that "Financial authorities are the legal instruments that empower and guide all public officers in carrying out government financial transactions."
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following is not one of the government financial transactions covered by financial authorities?
options: Disbursement of funds from major government funds.
Custody of government stores and assets.
Personal loan applications for public officers
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Personal loan applications for public officers). Financial Regulation 101 lists receipt, custody, accounting for revenue, procurement, custody, utilization of stores and assets, and disbursement of funds as government financial transactions. Personal loan applications are not included. 144
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Who must be notified of amounts collectable by a department's officers?
options: The Heads of Department or Divisions.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 230(i) requires that Heads of Department or Divisions be notified of amounts collectable by their officers.
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Who has the official authority to approve withdrawal of money from the Development Fund through a Provisional Development Fund General Warrant?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The President.). Financial Regulation 319 states: "the President must authorise the withdrawal of money from the Development fund as may be necessary through the issuance of a Provisional Development Fund General Warrant..."
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Who is personally responsible for the due performance of the financial duties of a ministry and for inaccuracies in the accounts rendered under that authority?
options: Only the Head of Finance and Accounts.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (All officers.). Financial Regulation 124 states, "All officers are personally and peculiarly accountable for the due performance of the financial duties of their ministries/extra-ministerial offices and other arms of government..." Part 2: Financial Regulations: Expenditure Classification and Control
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the consequence for an officer who makes, allows, or directs a disbursement without proper authority" according to official standards?
options: The officer will receive a warning.
The expenditure will be absorbed by the Contingencies Fund.
The officer will be personally liable for the amount involved.
The officers subordinates will be held accountable.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (They will be personally in charge for the amount involved.). Financial Regulation 401 (ii) states: "any officer making, allowing or directing any disbursement without proper authority must be personally accountable for the amount involved."
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which office is solely liable for unauthorized expenditure beyond the sum allocated?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 419 states that officers controlling votes are solely liable for unauthorized expenditure beyond the amount allocated.
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: What happens if an officer neglects to press for claims from private parties and prejudice results?
options: Takeover of the claim by the Accountant-General.
Liability of the neglecting officer for the resulting prejudice.
Exclusive loss borne by the private party.
questions.explanation: Where an officer neglects to press for claims from private parties and prejudice results, the neglecting officer is held in charge because official neglect should not prejudice the private party.
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: What happens when an officer controlling a vote incurs expenditure without proper authority?
options: The expenditure will be absorbed by the ministry.
The Accountant-General will rectify the error.
The Minister of Finance will issue a new warrant.
The officer will be held pecuniarily liable for the action.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The officer will be held pecuniarily in charge for his actions.). Financial Regulation 301 (which is referenced in 401) states: "Any officer controls a vote, or part thereof, who incurs expenditure without such authority does so on his sole accountability and will consequently be held peculiarly in charge for his actions."
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Why were accounting procedures standardized across federal ministries and self-accounting units?
options: Because accounting staff move frequently between offices.
To reduce the need for audits.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Due to the frequent movement of accounting staff.). Financial Regulation 503 states: "The frequent movement of accounting staff between ministries, extra/ministerial offices and other arms of Government calls for a degree of standardisation in the accounting procedures in use in all federal ministries and self accounting units."
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: What does the Finance (Control and Management) Act Cap 144, Laws of the Federation 1990 regulate?
options: Certain financial matters of the government.
Only the salaries of public officers.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Certain financial matters of the government.). Financial Regulation 104 states: "Certain financial matters of government are regulated by the laws of the Federation enacted by the National Assembly. These include the Finance (Control and Management) Act CAP 144 Laws of the Federation of Nigeria, 1990..."
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the role of the Head of Finance and Accounts in relation to compliance with the Financial Regulations" according to official standards?
options: To approve all variations in accounting procedures.
To secure compliance by all staff under the officers control and supervision.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To secure compliance by all staff under his control and supervision.). Financial Regulation 114(a) states: "ensuring compliance with Financial Regulations and the Accounting Code by all staff under his control and supervision." Financial Regulations: Payments Procedure
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which copies of disbursement vouchers must be signed in full in ink or ball pen by both the certifying officer and the payee?
options: Only the duplicate and triplicate copies.
Only the copy sent to the Auditor-General.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 606 requires only the originals of payment vouchers to be signed in full by the certifying officer and the payee.
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: which office is in charge for furnishing proof of identity if demand when a disbursement is being claimed?
options: The ministry, extra-ministerial office, or other arms of Government authorising payment.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The ministry, extra-ministerial office, or other arms of Government authorising payment.). Financial Regulation 613 states: "...it is the duty of the ministry, extra-ministerial office and other arms of Government authorising payment to furnish proof of identity if necessitate."
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: What should be presented to the Sub-Accounting Officer for inspection when payments are made to legal representatives?
options: A copy of the deceased's will only.
Authorities such as Powers of Attorney, Letters of Administration, etc.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Authorities such as Powers of Attorney, Letters of Administration, etc.). Financial Regulation 615 states: "When payments are to be made to legal representatives, authorities such as Powers of Attorney, Letters of Administration, etc. must be presented to the Sub Accounting Officer for inspection..."
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the minimum rank required for an officer empowered to draw cash or cheques on the strength of a Cheque Order Form" according to official standards?
options: Accountant II or Higher Executive Officer (Accounts).
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 628 provides that officers authorised to draw cash or cheques on the strength of a Cheque Order Form must not be below the rank of Accountant II or Higher Executive Officer (Accounts).
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Where should all Capital Accounts be maintained?
options: At the discretion of the Accounting Officer.
At the Central Bank of Nigeria or any other bank designated by the government.
In a foreign bank for international transactions.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Central Bank of Nigeria or any other designated banks by the government.). Financial Regulation 702 states: "All Capital Accounts must be maintained at the Central Bank of Nigeria or any other Banks as may be designated by the government of the federation and authorised by the Accountant-General."
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Who is responsible for furnishing certified specimen signatures of officers approved to sign cheques drawn on official accounts?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (All Accounting Officers.). Financial Regulation 705(i) states: "All Accounting Officers must furnish the details of commercial banks being used with certified specimen signatures of the officers authorised to sign cheques drawn on official accounts..."
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the rule on delegating authority to notify the bank of changes in empowered signatories" according to official standards?
options: Discretion left entirely to the Accounting Officer.
Non-delegation of the authority to notify the bank of signatory changes.
questions.explanation: The authority to notify the bank of changes in empowered signatories must not be delegated under the applicable financial regulation.
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: What follows if a government bank account is overdrawn?
options: Refund of any bank charges incurred by the accountable officer.
Reporting alone to the Minister of Finance.
Absorption of the overdraft by the bank without consequence.
questions.explanation: If a government bank account is overdrawn, the accountable officer must refund any bank charges incurred because of the overdraft.
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the implication if an officer pays public money into a private bank account" according to official standards?
options: The officer is deemed to have acted with fraudulent intention.
It is treated as a minor irregularity.
The money is simply transferred back to government later.
Only a warning is issued in the first instance.
questions.explanation: An officer who pays public money into a private bank account is deemed to have acted with fraudulent intention under the relevant financial regulation.
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: How often must officers permitted to keep bank accounts compare entries in bank statements with their cash books?
options: Weekly and at the close of each month.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Weekly and at the close of each month.). Financial Regulation 716 states: "All officers authorised to keep bank accounts must compare on- weekly basis, and at the close of each month, the entries appearing in the bank statements with those in their cash books..." 213
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Who should sign every request for official cheque books?
options: One of the empowered signatories to the account, countersigned by the Head of Accounts.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (One of the empower signatories to the account and countersigned by the Head of Accounts.). Financial Regulation 718 states: "Every request for official cheques books must be signed by one of the authorised signatories to the account and countersigned by the Head of Accounts."
questions.chapter: National Events & Key Personalities - Expansion Set

questions.question: Can cheques received by a Sub-Accounting Officer be endorsed or assigned to a third party?
options: Endorsement to a third party for urgent payments.
Absolute prohibition of endorsement or assignment to a third party.
Endorsement to another government entity only.
questions.explanation: Cheques received by a Sub-Accounting Officer must not be endorsed or assigned to a third party under any circumstances.
